## Absolutely awful app and terrible customer service
Absolutely awful app. Keeps crashing and for 4 months, my leader board froze and it took them 4 months to look into it, after which they had no idea of the complaint I made and I had to start all over again. Save your users time and patience and don't sell your course or community through them. They're absolutely shocking!

results.title: Circle.so Review: A brutally honest review of the community platform
results.content:
#### Default notifications seem to be a lot

Within my community onboarding I have to encourage members to go to their notifications settings and customise them right away. This is partly because Circle.so default notification settings seem a little intense. New members can easily become bombarded. Thankfully the notification settings for members are intuitive, yet robust, but still – I’d rather fix this issue at the source, with better default settings.

As you can see, most of these negatives are niggles, rather than deal breakers. Sure, some can be a little frustrating, but as you’re about to read, there’s a lot more that I love about Circle.so than what I don’t love.

Image 6: Circle.so notifications
## What I love about the Circle.so community platform [...] There’s also occasional bizarre bugs. My biggest pet peeve is that when I click ‘like’ on more than one member’s post in the home feed, it erratically jumps me down to the post below. I think it’s some weird anomaly where I have multiple comment sections open, but still – it’s one of those things that makes me die a little inside every time it happens. To be fair – Circle are typically great at resolving reported bugs, and I’ve been too lazy to report this one.

#### Their mobile app needs work [...] Circle.so Review: A brutally honest review of the community platform
Circle.so Review: A brutally honest review of the community platform

# Circle.so Review: A brutally honest review of the community platform

8 November 2021

Image 3

If you’re looking for your next, or even first community platform, then I hope this honest Circle.so review will help you.

Personally, I can’t stand super biased reviews, so, whilst full-disclosure; this review does contain affiliate links, I also made sure to include what I honestly don’t love about Circle, as well as who Circle definitely isn’t for.

results.title: Is Circle Any Good? An Honest Review for 2026 | Ruzuku
results.content:
The sunk-cost trap. A pattern across several reviews: creators who've built communities with 70+ members describe feeling unable to leave despite significant frustrations. One reviewer puts it directly: "sunk costs mean it's hard to leave" — they would never have signed up if they'd known basic functionality would break with no recourse to support. Another describes spending nearly $1,500 Canadian for the year with no phone or live support available before a program launch. The combination of migration difficulty and unresponsive support creates a lock-in dynamic that isn't about features — it's about the cost of starting over. [...] ## What Circle Users Say on Review Sites

As of April 2026, Circle has a2.5 out of 5 rating on Trustpilot from 20 reviews — with 55% being one-star. That's a small sample, but the complaints describe specific, documented patterns that are worth understanding. For the full cross-platform analysis, see our 2026 Course Platform Satisfaction Report. results.title: Is Circle ANY GOOD: A Brutally Honest Review
results.content:
makes. And it's really not just market and fluff. I've seen completion rates jump 40% when learners can immediately discuss what they've learned with their peers. But let's talk about what's missing because Circle ain't going to tell you. Firstly, there's no native quiz functionality if you want to assess learning. Well, you're building workarounds. Video hosting is included, but the live streaming quality is, and let's be diplomatic here, not broadcast grade. Multiple users have complained that the audio and visual quality really doesn't compete with Zoom. So, you're streaming to your community, but not really hosting a professional webinar. And here's something that's going to kind of shock you if you're coming from Kajjabi or Teachable. There is no native email marketing. You want to [...] what I'm going to show you in this video. Firstly, the architectural decision that Circle made that either makes it brilliant or completely wrong for you. There is no middle ground. Then, we'll examine whether their content features actually work or whether you're better off just duct taping Teachable to Discord. I'll reveal the pricing truth that Circle Berries in its footnotes, the one that could quietly drain thousands of dollars from your budget. And then finally, the integration capability that separates Circle Out from every wannabe competitor, but only if you're on the right plan. So, let's start out with a question that will probably annoy the Circle marketing team. Is Circle flexible enough to build a real production community that can service thousands of people, interests, and [...] # Is Circle ANY GOOD: A Brutally Honest Review
## Jono Bacon
12900 subscribers
282 likes

### Description
4913 views
Posted: 7 Nov 2025
Circle isn't a community platform...it's an engagement machine disguised as SaaS. This changes everything about whether you should use it.

After 27 years building communities for Fortune 500s and startups, I'm breaking down the architectural decision Circle made that makes it either brilliant or completely wrong for you.

In this video, you'll discover the pricing truth Circle buries in footnotes that could drain thousands from your budget, whether their content features actually work or you're better off with Teachable + Discord, and the integration capability that separates Circle from wannabe competitors (only on certain plans).
